system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

vertices

    ( 0  20  0)       // 3
    ( 0  0  1)        // 4
    (20  0  1)        // 5
    (20  20  1)       // 6
    ( 0  20  1)       // 7
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (40 40 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    fuel
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
        );
    }
    air
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
            (1 2 6 5)
        );
    }
    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
            (4 5 6 7)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     reactingFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          1e-6;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  8;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;

adjustTimeStep  yes;

maxCo           0.4;

maxDeltaT       1e-4;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;

    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,Yi_h)   Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,h)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,Ekp)    Gauss upwind;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "rho.*"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    p
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(Yi)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(Yi)Final"
    {
        $Yi;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='chemistryProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

chemistry       on;

initialChemicalTimeStep 1e-07;

odeCoeffs

    solver          seulex;
    absTol          1e-12;
    relTol          1e-01;
}

keyword species is undefined in dictionary "/pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_E2_E2models/runs/counterFlowFlame2D/1/constant/physicalProperties"

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_E2_E2models/runs/counterFlowFlame2D/1/constant/physicalProperties from line 12 to line 36.

    in file db/dictionary/dictionary.C at line 831.

<review_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error message is:

```
keyword species is undefined in dictionary ".../constant/physicalProperties"
```

This has persisted across 6 attempts. Every time the `species` dictionary is placed inside `multiComponentMixture`, it seems the fix doesn't take effect or there's a structural misunderstanding. Let me re-examine the current `physicalProperties` file more carefully.

Looking at the current file content provided, the `multiComponentMixture` block is:

```
multiComponentMixture
{
    specie        specie;
    multiComponentAssemblies
    {
        assem1
        {
            reactions   ("CH4 + 2O2 = CO2 + 2H2O");
            specie      ("CH4" "O2" "CO2" "H2O");
        }
    }
}
```

This block is indeed **missing** the `species` sub-dictionary. But crucially, the standalone top-level `species` dictionary IS present in the file. So the problem isn't that `species` is missing — it's that the `multiComponentMixture` constructor cannot find it.

However, given that 5 previous attempts to put `species` inside `multiComponentMixture` failed, perhaps the issue is different. Maybe in OpenFOAM 10, the `multiComponentMixture` expects the species list to be at the **top level** of the thermophysical properties dictionary, not inside the mixture block.

## New Approach

Let me try a completely different approach based on the reference tutorial case structure. In OpenFOAM 10's Foundation version, the `physicalProperties` file should have the `species` dictionary at the top level, and the `multiComponentMixture` should reference it indirectly through the `specie` entry combined with the `multiComponentAssemblies`.

The key insight: **remove the standalone `species` dictionary** and instead make sure the `multiComponentMixture` block has access to the species via the `multiComponentAssemblies` mechanism. Looking at the current file, the `multiComponentAssemblies` already lists the species explicitly in the `assem1` block. Perhaps OpenFOAM 10 can extract the species from the `multiComponentAssemblies` reactions/specie entries.

But since that hasn't worked in 5 attempts, let me try the opposite: keep the standalone `species` dictionary at the top level **but** ensure the `multiComponentMixture` block has a way to access it. The issue may be that `multiComponentMixture` looks for `species` in a different scope.

Actually, looking at the current file again, I think the real problem is simpler. The `multiComponentMixture` block is missing the `species` entry entirely. Even though there's a standalone `species` elsewhere, the mixture constructor may initialize before the standalone dict is processed, or it may only look within its own block.

Wait — the current file DOES have a standalone `species` dictionary. Why is it still failing? Let me check if the formatting is wrong. The standalone `species` uses flat format `CH4 16.043;` but maybe it needs sub-dict format.

## Proposed Fix

### 1. Modify `constant/physicalProperties` — Fix standalone `species` format

Change the standalone `species` dictionary from flat format to proper sub-dict format:

```
species
{
    CH4       { value 16.043; };
    O2        { value 31.999; };
    N2        { value 28.014; };
    H2O       { value 18.015; };
    CO2       { value 44.01; };
}
```

Keep everything else exactly as it is — including the `multiComponentMixture` block **without** an internal `species` entry. The standalone `species` dictionary should now be in the correct format.

### 2. Verify `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` has complete `janaf` data

Ensure all five species (CH4, O2, N2, H2O, CO2) have complete `janaf` thermodynamic blocks with `Cp`, `H`, `S`, and `T` coefficients.

### Summary

After 6 attempts, the standalone `species` dictionary was almost correct but likely had flat-format entries instead of sub-dict format. The `multiComponentMixture` block remains without an internal `species` entry. The fix is purely cosmetic: ensure the top-level `species` dictionary uses `{ value ...; }` sub-dictionary format for each species entry. Everything else stays as-is.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>do a counterflow flame simulation using OpenFOAM reactingFoam solver, employing a compressible flow solver for detailed combustion modeling. Define the computational domain as a 2D rectangular region with a width of 0.02 meters and a height of 0.02 meters. Use CH4, O2 and N2 as species. Include chemical kinetics using the reaction mechanism. velocity of fuel is 0.1 m/s and velocity of air is -0.1 m/s. Set the simulation runtime from 0 to 0.5 seconds with a write interval of 0.05 seconds and deltaT of 1e-6.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='constant/physicalProperties', changes='Change standalone species dictionary from flat format to proper sub-dict format: replace CH4 16.043 with CH4 { value 16.043; }, similarly for O2, N2, H2O, CO2 entries')]
